name: Subsurface Signal Lamp
oracle_text: Malfunction 1 (This permanent enters tapped with one malfunction counter on it. At the beginning of your upkeep, remove a malfunction counter from it.)\n{T}: Add one mana of any color.
flavor_text: It flickers three times before holding steady — once for each expedition that never made it back.

validation_errors: Line 1: "This permanent" should use "~" or "this" only in specific MTG contexts
Oracle text contains what looks like reminder text: "(This permanent enters tapped with one malfunction counter on it. At the beginning of your upkeep, remove a malfunction counter from it.)"

design_notes: A simple colorless mana rock at common that showcases Malfunction 1. At CMC 2, a mana rock that produces any color is strong, but entering tapped with a malfunction counter means it can't tap until the counter is removed at your next upkeep — effectively a two-turn delay before it's online (enters tapped turn 1, counter removed at upkeep of turn 2, then available to tap). This is significantly worse than Mind Stone or Arcane Signet, keeping it at common power level. The enters-tapped from Malfunction is meaningful here because the card has a tap ability, avoiding the checklist's "relevant enters-tapped only" concern. Malfunction 1 at common follows the scaling guidelines. The card supports the set's artifact theme, fixes mana for multicolor decks in draft, and interacts with counter-removal effects from the Cult of Science (UR) archetype like Relay Node Technician.
